connectivity_tips.tip: eSIM Options: Airalo, Holafly
Local SIMs: Viettel, Vinaphone, Mobifone
Wi-Fi: Widely available in hotels, cafes, and restaurants
eSIM is easiest for families
There are 3 main SIM Card providers in Vietnam: Viettel, Vinaphone, and Mobifone. Viettel is recommended for best coverage.
Expect to pay between 150,000-350,000 VND (Rs. 500 - Rs. 1200) for a SIM card and then additional for a prepaid data package
The best place to buy a sim card is at the airport kiosks
Even if you do not purchase an international roaming plan for your current India number, remember to activate roaming so that you receive messages (important for things like OTPs)
There are 3 main SIM Card providers: Viettel, Vinaphone, and Mobifone. Viettel is recommended for best coverage
Expect to pay between 150,000-350,000 VND (Rs. 500 - Rs. 1200) for a SIM card plus additional for prepaid data package
Best place to buy a sim card is at the airport kiosks
Activate roaming on your India number to receive messages like OTPs even without international roaming plan
Expect to pay between 150,000-350,000 VND (Rs. 500 - Rs. 1200) for a SIM card and additional for prepaid data package.
The best place to buy a sim card is at the airport kiosks.
Activate roaming on your India number to receive messages for OTPs even without international roaming plan.
Viettel – The largest and most reliable network with excellent coverage even in rural areas like Sapa and the Mekong Delta
Vinaphone – Good network coverage in cities and tourist destinations
Mobifone – Reliable in major cities with competitive data plans

You can easily purchase a tourist SIM card at Noi Bai International Airport (Hanoi), Da Nang International Airport, Tan Son Nhat International Airport (Ho Chi Minh City), official telecom stores, and convenience stores
Typical Cost: Approx. VND 150,000 – 300,000 (INR 500 – 1000) depending on the data package. Most tourist SIM cards include 10–20 GB of data valid for 15–30 days
If your phone supports eSIM, you may prefer activating an international eSIM before departure. Popular travel eSIM providers include Airalo, Holafly, and Nomad
There are 3 main SIM Card providers: Viettel, Vinaphone, and Mobifone. Viettel is recommended for best coverage.
Expect to pay between 150,000-350,000 VND (₹500-₹1200) for a SIM card plus additional for prepaid data package.
Best place to buy a sim card is at the airport kiosks.
Activate roaming on your India number to receive messages for OTPs even if not purchasing international roaming plan.
Expect to pay between 150,000-350,000 VND (₹500-1200) for a SIM card plus additional for prepaid data package
Even without international roaming plan, activate roaming to receive messages for OTPs
Recommended Carriers: Viettel, Vinaphone, Mobifone, with Viettel being the top recommendation
Buy SIM cards at Da Nang Airport (after arrival) or convenience stores
Cost: ~INR 500–700 for 7–10 days with unlimited data
4G coverage is strong in Da Nang, Hoi An, and even along Hai Van Pass
Wi-Fi is available in most cafés, hotels, and even markets
Use eSIM platforms like Airalo or Holafly if your phone supports digital SIMs (~INR 1,200–1,500 for 10 days)
Activate the SIM at the airport with help from staff to ensure APN settings are configured correctly
There are 3 main SIM Card providers: Viettel, Vinaphone, and Mobifone. Viettel is recommended for best coverage within Vietnam.
Expect to pay between 150,000-350,000 VND (Rs. 500 - Rs. 1200) for a SIM card plus additional for prepaid data package.
Even without international roaming plan, activate roaming to receive messages for OTPs.
There are 3 main SIM Card providers in Vietnam: Viettel, Vinaphone, and Mobifone. Viettel is recommended as it has best coverage for traveling within Vietnam

safety_tips.tip: Avoid nighttime travel in remote or poorly lit areas, especially outside Hoi An or Son Tra
Avoid beaches after dark – avoid isolated areas
Be careful with motorbike traffic – always use pedestrian crossings carefully
Watch out for taxi meter rigging – Always use trusted apps like Grab for safe pricing
Be aware of currency confusion – VND has high denominations; double-check notes before handing over
Don't carry all your cash/cards in one place
Use hotel safes for passports and valuables
Be cautious at night markets; petty theft is rare but possible
Carry a copy of your passport when exploring
Watch belongings in crowded markets
Drink bottled water only
Cross roads slowly & confidently
Keep valuables in crossbody bags or money belts, especially in crowded areas
Avoid carrying passports daily — keep copies instead
Use hotel safes for passports, cash, and jewelry
Stick to well-lit areas at night
Apply insect repellent during evenings and near water bodies
Refrain from taking cabs which are not from recommended companies (Grab, Uber, Vinasun, Mai linh) to avoid scams
Unless particularly known for their veg or chicken/seafood options, avoid visiting the sidewalk restaurants
Avoid nighttime travel in isolated or poorly lit areas, especially outside busy tourist zones
Beaches after dark – Stick to well-lit areas and avoid secluded sections late at night
Busy roads and motorbike traffic – Traffic can be chaotic; cross roads slowly and steadily rather than rushing
Avoid unmarked taxis. Use trusted ride-hailing apps like Grab for transparent pricing
Vietnamese Dong has large denominations. Always double-check the amount before paying
Bargaining is normal in markets; compare prices and negotiate politely
Avoid carrying all cash and cards in one place
Use the hotel safe for passports and valuables
Carry a printed or digital copy of your passport while exploring
Stay mindful of personal belongings in busy markets and nightlife areas
Refrain from taking cabs which are not from Grab, Uber, Vinasun, or Mai linh to avoid scams
Refrain from taking cabs which are not from Grab, Uber, Vinasun, or Mai linh companies to avoid scams
Refrain from taking cabs which are not from approved companies (Grab, Uber, Vinasun, Mai linh) to avoid scams

health_tips.tip: Carry personal prescription medications
Pack basic first-aid kit with band-aids and antiseptic wipes
Bring motion sickness tablets for Halong Bay cruise
Pack ORS packets and antihistamines for insect bites
Get travel insurance with 10,000 USD coverage per person. Recommendation - TATA AIG
Carry mosquito repellent
Bring doctors prescriptions/travel prescriptions for medicines (except over the counter meds)
Carry doctors prescriptions/travel prescriptions for medicines (except over the counter meds)
Get travel insurance with 10,000 USD coverage per person. Recommendation - TATA AIG.
Carry doctors prescriptions/travel prescriptions for medicines (except over the counter meds).
Bring mosquito repellent.
It is advisable to consult your doctor 4–6 weeks before travel regarding recommended vaccines including Hepatitis A, Typhoid, Tetanus, and routine immunizations
Hepatitis A – Recommended for most travelers as it can be contracted through contaminated food or water
Typhoid – Recommended if you plan to eat local food or street food
Tetanus – Ensure your routine booster is up to date
Stay well hydrated, especially in warm and humid coastal cities like Da Nang and Ho Chi Minh City
Use sunscreen, sunglasses, and a hat when sightseeing during the day
It is always helpful to carry a small personal medical kit while traveling
Take it slow during the first day to adjust to the climate and travel fatigue
Carry mosquito repellent.
Bring doctors prescriptions/travel prescriptions for medicines (except over the counter meds).
Travel Insurance recommended - get 10,000 USD coverage per person. Recommendation: TATA AIG
Bring mosquito repellent
Hepatitis A vaccination recommended for all travelers
Hepatitis B vaccination recommended, especially if staying long or visiting rural areas
Typhoid vaccination recommended if you're exploring local markets, street food
Ensure Tetanus & Diphtheria booster is updated (every 10 years)
Japanese Encephalitis vaccination optional, for longer stays or visits to rural areas during monsoon
Rabies vaccination not essential for city travelers but consider if interacting with animals or visiting rural areas
No Yellow Fever requirement for travelers from India
Carry mosquito repellent, sunscreen, and hydration salts—very useful for tropical travel
Most pharmacies in Da Nang are well-stocked, but carry your essential meds from home, especially prescription ones
Carry doctor's prescriptions/travel prescriptions for medicines (except over the counter meds)

transport_options.description: Convenient ride-hailing service for getting around the city
23 minutes from Da Nang International Airport to hotel
Trusted ride-sharing app for safe pricing
Luxury cruise experience through limestone karsts and emerald waters
Boat cruises through Halong Bay's limestone karsts and islands
Private air-conditioned vehicle transfer from Noi Bai International Airport to hotel
Areas of Hanoi are incredibly walkable
Dinner cruises along the Saigon River with city skyline views
Traditional bamboo basket boats for coconut forest tours
Free shuttle bus service for rides to and from Phu Quoc Night Market. Take Vin Bus 17 or 19. Bus frequency every 10-20 mins. Runs from 6 am to midnight.
Cable car system to reach Fansipan Mountain peak
Ride hailing app - you will need a data pack for this to book Taxis on the go. You can set up your account in India and set up your credit card as a payment option, before you leave on vacation
Ride hailing app - you will need a data pack for this to book Taxis on the go. You can use your existing account
Ride hailing app - recommended for ease, affordability and reliability. Requires data pack to book taxis

Ride hailing app - most recommended for ease, affordability and reliability
Ride-hailing app for booking taxis, bike rides, and food delivery with transparent pricing
Available throughout cities, recommended to use marked taxis or ride-hailing apps
Comfortable shared transfer service between cities like Hanoi to Sapa
Connecting major cities like Hanoi, Da Nang, and Ho Chi Minh City
Ride hailing app - most recommended for ease, affordability and reliability
